title: Shared-prefix scorer with hard-negative rank auxiliary

queries: shared prefix candidate scoring multiclass language model label ranking
hard negative ranking loss candidate scorer MRR

evidence.why_now: EXP108 showed that the scalable shared-prefix scorer preserved item-incoterms near the strict baseline, but did not become a promotable bundle. EXP162 showed that hard-negative ranking pressure improves the default scorer's salt-side MRR band but remains clearly below baseline. EXP126 showed that autoregressive shortlist reranking is too expensive. The next justified candidate is therefore to combine the strongest scalable candidate-token information path from EXP108 with the direct MRR-aligned hard-negative supervision from EXP162.

hypothesis: If hard-negative rank pressure is directionally useful but the default scorer lacks enough candidate-token information, then applying the same margin-free hard-negative auxiliary to the scalable shared-prefix candidate-token scorer should preserve the near-baseline salt-side signal from EXP108 while giving the true candidate stronger pressure over the hardest wrong candidate. This remains finetune-only, candidate-aware, non-autoregressive, and avoids exhaustive O(L*C) rollout.
notes: Keep Stage 3 finetune-only; do not use --pretrain.
This candidate is not a token-stats, pooled-label, or residual-strength variant.
It combines two prior-ablation signals: EXP108's scalable shared-prefix candidate-token scorer and EXP162's direct hard-negative ranking pressure.
The hard-negative auxiliary is active only for autocomplete multiclass; user-churn and user-ltv remain no-op drift controls.
Exclude GPU0 for this candidate only because EXP162 showed a current pre-eval SIGTERM when user-churn used GPU0 as local_rank 0; pipeline_config keeps gpu_exclude empty globally.
Launched on 2026-05-09 through the local Stage 3 launcher.

Resolved packed batch-equivalent placement at launch time: user-churn -> lab25211 GPUs 5,6 with per-rank batch 2 in wave 0; user-ltv -> lab25211 GPU 5 with per-rank batch 2 in wave 1; item-incoterms -> lab25211 GPUs 6,7 with per-rank batch 2 in wave 1.
First post-launch status check showed EXP165 window up in DB-load startup; EXP166 and EXP167 are correctly waiting for wave 1; orchestrator recommendation: keep_watching.
Completed on 2026-05-10 with global verdict: failed.
Result under replay-aware effective deltas: user-churn better, user-ltv neutral, item-incoterms worse.

EXP167 item-incoterms was manually killed after the 4096-step evaluation because it was the only remaining active task, throughput was about 1.26 train steps/sec on GPUs 6,7 with a projected 6+ hours remaining, and best MRR was still clearly below baseline.
Throughput conclusion: despite being non-O(L*C) in intent, the shared-prefix scorer is not acceptable for routine Stage 3 screening in its current implementation.
Scientific conclusion: adding the hard-negative ranking auxiliary to the shared-prefix scorer did not preserve the near-baseline EXP108 salt-side behavior.
